description: Celebrate Women Equality Day on 26th August by joining our interactive workshop, designed to promote diversity, equity, and inclusion. This event, organized by our non-profit, is part of our commitment to activism and global observances & celebrations that empower women. The workshop will provide a platform to discuss and learn about the ongoing struggle for women's equality. It will also highlight the importance of inclusive practices in our daily lives. Don't miss this opportunity to contribute to a more equitable world. Join us and be a part of the change!
short_description: Join our Women Equality Day workshop on 26th August. Organized by a non-profit, it focuses on diversity, equity, and inclusion. Learn, engage, and take action towards women's equality. Celebrate global observances of women's rights and activism. Let's bridge the gender gap together.

designer.description: In addition to my expertise in UI/UX design and Digital Marketing, I possess a strong background in creating strategic pathways that guide users towards their desired goals. With a deep understanding of customer journeys and effective conversion techniques, I excel in designing optimized user experiences that drive engagement and facilitate desired actions. Drawing on 5 years of experience, I leverage my knowledge of user behavior, persuasive design principles, and data-driven insights to create intuitive and seamless pathways that enhance user interactions and improve conversion rates. My ability to conceptualize, design, and implement these strategic frameworks ensures that users have a compelling and frictionless experience, resulting in increased customer satisfaction and business growth.

html_data:
Join Our Workshop

WORKSHOP

Let's Break
Boundaries Together

26 August, 2021 | 2pm EST

Meet Our Speakers

John Doe

CTO

Ellen Davidson

Entrepreneur

Steven Max

Entrepreneur

 
 

“ I Want Every Girl to Know That Her Voice Can Change the World “

 

Malala Fund

 

Women Were/Are/Will Always be Powerful

Jane Austen

 

Let's start with a literary figure who has had more influence on British culture than she would ever have known during her relatively short life.

These were Sense and Sensibility, Pride and Prejudice, Mansfield Park, and Emma. Northanger Abbey and Persuasion were published after she died - and they are loved all over the world.

While she was alive, she actually published her books anonymously, so nobody knew her as a writer. It is widely accepted that she never got the credit she deserved until after her death.

Now, just over 200 years since she died, millions of people carry Jane around in their pockets every single day. That's because her face is now on the new £10 note as a way of marking just what an enormous impact her work still has today.

Let’s increase awareness through our workshop for women equality day.

APPLY NOW
